title: fagraea longiflora
intro: Fagraea longiflora, also known as the Frangipani or Frangipani tree, is a tropical flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin infections, and has also been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The herbal preparations of Fagraea longiflora include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, and it is often used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
